calendar.matters:
Petition For Instruction And If Necessary, Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Nov-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frank Cialone for and with Gary Schefsky.
Gary Cohen for Laurie Schefsky and Steve Schefsky.

Mr. Cohen stated that his Client's do not wish to go forward with redevelopment, that they understand that the Trustee has already expended money on entitlements, that entitlement items are valuable and that the plan was that dissolution would be filed right away. Mr. Cohen also stated that the Trust would own 100% of the property, that the only thing they disagree to is $15,000.00 in fees to Gary, and that if they are willing to waive the oversight fee, his Clients are okay with it. Mr. Cialone stated that there is no request for a fee for Gary, that the end game is dissolution and then a buyout, and that the agreements have not been forthcoming. Mr. Cialone also spoke of the possibility of getting a receiver appointed to wind down the partnership. Mr. Schefsky stated that he is not seeking an oversight fee in this petition and that he proposed fees at $175.00 an hour in a letter. The Petition for Instruction, etc. is taken under submission. The Court encourages the parties to communicate.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
